title: Simple Threshold Alerting
description: Given a time series of metric values (timestamp\tvalue per line), detect alert conditions where the metric exceeds a threshold for at least N consecutive readings. Return the starting timestamp of each alert window. Overlapping or adjacent windows that are not separated by a below-threshold value count as one alert starting at the first breach.
constraints: Alert fires when 'consecutive' readings in a row exceed 'threshold'
Return the timestamp of the first reading in each qualifying run
Threshold defaults to 80.0, consecutive defaults to 3
Values and threshold are floats; a reading AT the threshold (equal) does not trigger

examples.explanation: Timestamps 2,3,4 all exceed 80.0 (3 consecutive readings), triggering an alert at timestamp 2. The reading at timestamp 6 equals 80.0 (not strictly above) so no second alert fires.

starter_code.python:
def threshold_alerts(log_content: str, threshold: float = 80.0, consecutive: int = 3) -> list[int]:
    pass

test_cases.description: Three consecutive above-threshold values trigger one alert at ts=2
All values below threshold, no alerts
Two separate alert windows each with 3+ consecutive values
